text.title: Security by Design Feature Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules — one per STRIDE letter — that every per-feature security-by-design checklist MUST satisfy before architecture review approval.

rule.statement: (S) Spoofing: checklist MUST answer "who can claim to be the user/service in this feature, and what proves identity?" Output: identity-claim mechanism (auth token / mTLS cert / signed request) + verification step. Decision MUST be one of {mitigated, accepted+rationale, deferred+ticket}.
rule.rationale: Identity assumptions silently leak between features. Naming the claim mechanism per feature catches "we'll just trust the JWT" gaps.

rule.statement: (T) Tampering: checklist MUST answer "what data does this feature persist or transmit, and what prevents modification in flight or at rest?" Output: integrity mechanism (TLS / signed payload / row hash / write-once log). Decision MUST be one of {mitigated, accepted+rationale, deferred+ticket}.
rule.rationale: Features that "just read from the DB" still tamper risk if upstream writes are not authenticated. Forces upstream-of-feature thinking.

rule.statement: (R) Repudiation: checklist MUST answer "what user/admin actions in this feature need to be non-repudiable?" Output: audit-log entry (who, what, when, source-IP/agent) + retention period. Decision MUST be one of {mitigated, accepted+rationale, deferred+ticket}.
rule.rationale: Audit logging is the cheapest mitigation that nobody adds until after the first incident. Per-feature prompt is the only reliable way to install it.

rule.statement: (I) Information Disclosure: checklist MUST answer "what new PII/secrets/internal state is exposed by this feature, intentionally or as side-effect (logs, error messages, telemetry)?" Output: data-class table + redaction policy. Decision MUST be one of {mitigated, accepted+rationale, deferred+ticket}.
rule.rationale: Logs and error messages are the #1 PII-leak vector. STRIDE-heavyweight workshops catch it; lightweight feature pass catches it earlier and cheaper.

rule.statement: (D) Denial of Service: checklist MUST answer "what unbounded input, recursion, or downstream call does this feature introduce, and what bounds it?" Output: rate-limit / quota / timeout / circuit-breaker that applies. Decision MUST be one of {mitigated, accepted+rationale, deferred+ticket}.
rule.rationale: Features add accidental DoS vectors via fan-out, unbounded queries, or new third-party API calls. Catching at design time is 100x cheaper than at incident time.

rule.statement: (E) Elevation of Privilege: checklist MUST answer "does this feature let any actor act with privilege they didn't have before — directly or via path traversal, role inheritance, deserialization, or template injection?" Output: privilege-boundary diff + enforcement point. Decision MUST be one of {mitigated, accepted+rationale, deferred+ticket}.
rule.rationale: EoP is the bug class with the worst blast radius and the easiest to miss in a feature that "just adds an admin toggle." Forcing the question stops the toggle from becoming RCE.

example.title: Feature STM excerpt
example.text: feature: bulk-export-csv STRIDE: {S: mitigated(JWT+rate-limit), T: mitigated(signed row hash), R: mitigated(audit-log 7y), I: deferred(redaction-policy in TICKET-2114), D: mitigated(rate-limit 5/min/user, query timeout 30s), E: accepted(admin role required; rationale: scoped to org)} reviewer: @sec-lead 2026-05-20 decision: APPROVE WITH FOLLOW-UP (I-deferred)
